verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We rejoice at your numbers, for you have come together with readiness and in greater numbers than we could have hoped.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This it is that delights and consoles us in all the labors and dangers of this life, your love towards God, and pious zeal, and assured hope, and fervor of spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You heard when the psalm was read, “that the needy and poor man cries to God in this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is the voice, as you have often heard, and ought to remember, not of one man, and yet of one man; not of one, because the faithful are many—many grains groaning amid the chaff diffused throughout the whole world—but of one, because all are members of Christ, and thus one body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This people, then, poor and needy, does not know to rejoice with the world: its grief is within, and its joy is within, where no one sees but He who listens to him who groans, and crowns him who hopes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The rejoicing of the world is vanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With great expectation is it hoped for and it cannot, when it comes, be held fast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this day which is a day of rejoicing in this city to the lost, to-morrow will, of course, cease to be; nor will they themselves be the same tomorrow that they are to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And all things pass away, fly away, and vanish like smoke; and woe to those who love such things!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For every soul follows what it loves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All flesh is grass, and all the goodliness thereof as the flower of the field: the grass withereth, the flower fadeth; but the word of the Lord abideth forever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold what thou must love if thou dost desire to abide for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou hadst this to reply: How can I apprehend the word of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, beloved, let it belong to our neediness and poverty to grieve for those who seem to themselves to abound.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For their joy is as that of madmen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as a madman rejoices for the most part in his madness, and laughs, and grieves over him who is in his senses, so let us, beloved, if we have received the medicine coming from heaven, because we all were madmen, as if made whole, because those things which we did love we do not love,—let us, I say, groan unto God for those who are yet in madness, for He is able to save them also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there is need that they should look upon themselves and be displeased with themselves: to behold they desire, and to behold themselves they have not known.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if they for a little turn their eyes upon themselves, they see their own confusion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But until this take place, let our pursuits be different, let the recreations of our souls be different; our grief avails more than their joy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As far as regards the number of the brethren, it is difficult to conceive that any one of the men should have been carried away by that celebration; but as regards the number of the sisters, it grieves us, and this is a greater cause for grief, that they do not rather repair to the Church, whom if not fear, modesty at all events ought to deter from the public scene.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He see to this who sees it; and may His mercy be present to heal all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us who have come together feed upon the feast of God, and let our joy be His word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He has invited us to His gospel, and He is our food, than whom nothing is sweeter, if only a man have a healthy palate in his heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I imagine, beloved brethren, that you remember that this Gospel is read in order in suitable portions; and I think that it has not escaped you what has lately been treated of, specially the recent matters concerning John and the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Concerning John, namely, what new thing he learned concerning the Lord by means of the dove, although he had already known the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this was discovered by the inspiration of the Spirit of God, that John indeed already knew the Lord, but that the Lord Himself was to baptize, that the power of baptizing He would not transfer from Himself to any one, this he learned by means of the dove, because it was said to him, “On whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, this is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “This is He”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not another, although by means of another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why by means of a dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many things were said, and I am not able, nor is there need that I should go over all;—principally, however, to denote peace, because also the trees which were baptized outside, because the dove found in them fruit, it brought to the ark, as you remember the dove sent out by Noah from the ark, which floated on the flood and was washed by baptism, was not submerged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, then, it was sent forth, it brought an olive branch; but it had not leaves alone, it had also fruit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, we ought to wish for our brethren who are baptized outside, that they may have fruit; the dove will not permit them to remain outside, but bring them back to the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the whole of fruit is charity, without which a man is nothing, whatever else he have.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this, which is most fully said by the apostle, we have mentioned and recounted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he says, “Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass or a tinkling cymbal; and though I should have all knowledge, and know all mysteries, and have all prophecy, and should have all faith” (but in what sense did he say all faith?), “so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And though I should distribute all my goods to the poor, and though I should give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in no manner are they able to say that they have charity who divide unity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These things were said: let us see what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John bare record because he saw.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What record did he bear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That this is the Son of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It behoved, then, that He should baptize who is God’s only Son, not His adopted son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Adopted sons are the ministers of the only Son: the only Son has power; the adopted, the ministry.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the case that a minister baptizes who does not belong to the number of sons, because he lives evilly and acts evilly, what is our consolation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This is He which baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The next day, John stood, and two of his disciples; and looking upon Jesus as He walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Assuredly, in a special sense, the Lamb; for the disciples were also called lambs: “Behold, I send you as lambs in the midst of wolves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were also called light: “Ye are the light of the world;” but in another sense is He called so, concerning whom it was said, “That was the true light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner was He called the dove in a special sense, alone without stain, without sin; not one whose sins have been washed away, but One who never had stain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because John said concerning the Lord, “Behold the Lamb of God,” was not John himself a lamb?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he not a holy man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he not the friend of the Bridegroom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, with a special meaning, said John of Him, “This is the Lamb of God;” because solely by the blood of this Lamb alone could men be redeemed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, if we acknowledge our price, that it is the blood of the Lamb, who are they who this day celebrate the festival of the blood of I know not what woman, and how ungrateful are they!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The gold was snatched, they say, from the ear of a woman, and the blood ran, and the gold was placed on a pair of scales or on a balance, and the advantage was much on the side of the blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the blood of a woman was sufficiently weighty to outweigh the gold, what power to outweigh the world has the blood of the Lamb by whom the world was made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, indeed, that spirit, I know not who, was pacified by the blood that he should depress the weight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Impure spirits knew that Jesus Christ would come, they had heard of His coming from the angels, they had heard of it from the prophets, and they expected it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if they were not expecting it, why did they exclaim, “What have we to do with Thee? art Thou come before the time to destroy us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We know who Thou art; the Holy One of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They expected that He would come, but they were ignorant of the time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what have you heard in the psalm regarding Jerusalem?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For Thy servants have taken pleasure in her stones, and will pity the dust thereof.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou shall arise,” says he, “and have mercy upon Zion: for the time is come that Thou wilt have mercy upon her.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the time came for God to have mercy, the Lamb came.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of a Lamb whom wolves fear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of a Lamb is it who, when slain, slew a lion?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the devil is called a lion, going about and roaring, seeking whom he may devour.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the blood of the Lamb the lion was vanquished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold the spectacles of Christians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is more: they with the eyes of the flesh behold vanity, we with the eyes of the heart behold truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not think, brethren, that our Lord God has dismissed us without spectacles; for if there are no spectacles, why have ye come together to-day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, what we have said you saw, and you exclaimed; you would not have exclaimed if you had not seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this is a great thing to see in the whole world, the lion vanquished by the blood of the Lamb: members of Christ delivered from the teeth of the lions, and joined to the body of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore some spirit or other contrived the counterfeit that His image should be bought for blood, because he knew that the human race was at some time to be redeemed by the precious blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For evil spirits counterfeit certain shadows of honor to themselves, that they may deceive those who follow Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So much so, my brethren, that those who seduce by means of amulets, by incantations, by the devices of the enemy, mingle the name of Christ with their incantations: because they are not now able to seduce Christians, so as to give them poison they add some honey, that by means of the sweet the bitter may be concealed, and be drunk to ruin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So much so, that I know that the priest of that Pilleatus was sometimes in the habit of saying, Pilleatus himself also is a Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why so, brethren, unless that they were not able otherwise to seduce Christians?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not, then, seek Christ elsewhere than where Christ wished Himself to be preached to you; and as He wished Himself to be preached to you, in that fashion hold Him fast, in that manner write Him on your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a wall against all the assaults, and against all the snares of the enemy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not fear, he does not tempt unless he has been permitted; it is certain that he does nothing unless permitted or sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is sent as an evil angel by a power holding him in control: he is permitted when he asks anything; and this, brethren, does not take place unless that the just may be tried, the unjust punished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, dost thou fear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Walk in the Lord thy God; be thou assured, what He does not wish thee to suffer thou dost not suffer; what He permits thee to suffer is the scourge of one correcting, not the punishment of one condemning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are being educated for an eternal inheritance, and do we spurn to be scourged?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, if a boy were to refuse the punishment of cuffs or stripes from his father, would he not be called proud, incorrigible, ungrateful towards paternal discipline?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And for what does an earthly father educate his son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That he may not lose the temporal things which he has acquired for him, which he has collected for him, which he does not wish him to lose, which he who leaves them cannot retain eternally.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not teach a son with whom he is to possess, but one who is to possess after him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, if a father teaches a son who is to succeed him, and teaches him also that he will have to pass through all these things, in same way as he who is admonishing him is destined to pass through them, how do you wish that He educate us, our Father to whom we are not to succeed, but to whom we are to approach, and with whom we are to abide eternally in an inheritance which does not decay nor die, and which no storms can desolate?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is Himself both the inheritance and the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall we possess Him, and ought we not to undergo training?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us hear the instruction of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When our head aches, let us not have recourse to the superstitious intercessor, to the diviners and remedies of vanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, shall I not mourn over you?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Daily do I find these things; and what shall I do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not yet have I persuaded Christians that their hope ought to be placed in God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, if one dies to whom one of these remedies has been given (and how many have died with remedies, and how many have lived without them!), with what confidence does the spirit go forth to God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has lost the sign of Christ, and has received the sign of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps he may say that he has not lost the sign of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou canst have, then, the sign of Christ along with the sign of the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ does not desire community of ownership, but He desires to possess alone what He has purchased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has bought at so great a price that He may possess alone: thou makest Him the partner of that devil to whom thou didst sell thyself by thy sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Woe to the double-hearted,” to those who in their hearts give part to God and part to the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God, being angry that the devil has part there, departs, and the devil will possess the whole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not in vain, therefore, says the apostle, “Neither give place to the devil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us know the Lamb, then, brethren; let us know our price.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “John stood, and two of his disciples.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold two of John’s disciples: since John, the friend of the Bridegroom, was such as he was, he sought not his own glory, but bore witness to the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did he wish that his disciples should remain with him and not follow the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather he himself showed hisdisciples whom they should follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they accounted of him as though he were the lamb; and he said, “Why do you give heed to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am not the lamb; behold the Lamb of God,” of whom also he had already said, Behold the Lamb of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what benefit does the Lamb of God confer upon us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold,” he says, “who taketh away the sin of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The two who were with John followed Him when they heard this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see what follows: “Behold the Lamb of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This John said, and the two disciples heard him speak, and followed Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then Jesus turned and saw them following, and saith unto them, “What seek ye?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they said, “Rabbi (that is to say, being interpreted, Master), where dwellest Thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not follow Him in such manner as that they should cleave to Him; for it is plain when they clave unto Him, for He called them from the ship.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For one of the two was Andrew, as you have just heard, and Andrew was the brother of Peter; and we know from the Gospel that the Lord called Peter and Andrew from the ship, saying, “Come ye after me, and I will make you fishers of men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And from that time they clave unto Him, so as not to go away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On the present occasion these two followed Him, not as those who were not again to leave Him, but to see where He dwelt, and to fulfill the Scripture: “Let thy foot wear out the threshold of His doors; arise to come to Him continually, and be instructed in His precepts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He showed them where He dwelt: they came and remained with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What a blessed day they spent, what a blessed night!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who can make known to us those things which they heard from the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us also build in our heart, and make a house into which He may come and teach us, and have converse with us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “What seek ye?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They said unto Him, “Rabbi (which is to say, being interpreted, Master), where dwellest Thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says to them, Come and see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they came and saw where He dwelt, and abode with Him that day: and it was about the tenth hour.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we think that it did in no wise pertain to the evangelist to tell us what hour it was?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it possible that he wished us to give heed to nothing in that, to inquire after nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was the tenth hour.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That number signifies the law, because the law was given in ten commandments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the time had come for the law to be fulfilled by love, because it could not be fulfilled by the Jews by fear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence the Lord says, “I am not come to destroy the law, but to fulfill.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suitably, then, at the tenth hour did these two follow Him, at the testimony of the friend of the Bridegroom, and that He at the tenth hour heard “Rabbi (which is interpreted, Master).”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If at the tenth hour the Lord heard Rabbi, and the tenth number pertains to the law, the master of the law is no other than the giver of the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no one say that one gave the law, and that another teaches the law: for the same teaches it who gave it; He is the Master of His own law, and teaches it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And mercy is in His tongue, therefore mercifully teacheth He the law, as it is said regarding wisdom, “The law and mercy doth she carry in her tongue.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not fear that thou art not able to fulfill the law, flee to mercy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou canst not fulfill the law, make use of that covenant, make use of the bond, make use of the prayers which the heavenly One, skilled in the law, has ordained and composed for you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those who have a cause, and wish to supplicate the emperor, seek for some one skilled in the law, and trained in the schools, to compose their petition for them; lest perchance, if they ask in an unbecoming manner, they not only do not obtain what they seek, but get punishment instead of a benefit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, the apostles sought to petition, and could not find how to approach the Emperor God, they said unto Christ, “Lord, teach us to pray;” that is to say, “O thou who art our skilled One in the law, our Assessor, yea, the Concessor of God, compose for us prayers.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord taught them from the book of the celestial law, taught them how to pray; and in that which He taught, He laid down a certain condition: “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou seekest not according to the law, thou becomest guilty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou not tremble before the Emperor, having become guilty?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Offer the sacrifice of humility, offer the sacrifice of mercy; pray, saying, Forgive me, for I also forgive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou sayest, do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what wilt thou do? whither wilt thou go if thou hast lied in thy prayers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not as it is said in the forum, thou shalt lose the benefit of the rescript; but thou shall not obtain a rescript.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is the law of the forum that he who shall have lied in his petition shall derive no benefit from that which he has obtained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this among men, because a man can be deceived: the emperor might have been deceived, when thou didst address to him thy petition; for thou saidest what thou wouldest, and he to whom thou didst speak knew not whether it was true or false; he sent thee away to thy adversary to be confuted if possible, so that if before the judge thou shouldest be convicted of falsehood (because he was not able not to grant the rescript, not knowing whether thou hadst lied), thou shouldest lose the benefit of the rescript, in the place to which thou hadst taken it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But God, who knows whether thou liest or speakest the truth, does not cause thee to lose in the judgment the benefit, but does not permit thee to obtain it, because thou hast dared to lie to the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, wilt thou do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To fulfill the law in every part, so as to offend in nothing, is difficult: the condition of guilt is therefore certain; wilt thou refuse to use the remedy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, my brethren, what a remedy the Lord hath provided for the sicknesses of the soul!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thy head aches, we praise thee if thou placest the gospel at thy head, instead of having recourse to an amulet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so far has human weakness proceeded, and so lamentable is the estate of those who have recourse to amulets, that we rejoice when we see a man who is upon his bed, and tossed about with fevers and pains, placing his hope on nothing else than that the gospel lies at his head; not because it is done for this purpose, but because the gospel is preferred to amulets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, it is placed at the head to allay the pain of the head, is it not placed at the heart to heal it from sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it be done then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let what be done?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it be placed at the heart, let the heart be healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is well,—well that thou shouldest have no further care regarding the safety of the body, than to ask it from God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He knows that it will do thee good, He will give it thee; if He give it not to thee, it would not have profited thee to have it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many are sick in bed, and for that reason are innocent! for if they were to recover, they would go forth to commit acts of wickedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To how many is health an injury!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The robber who goes forth to the narrow path to slay a man, how much better for him would it have been to have been sick!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he who rises by night to dig through his neighbor’s wall, how much better for him to be tossed by fever!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he were ill, he would have been comparatively innocent; being well, he is guilty of wickedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is known, then, to God what is expedient for us: let us make this only our endeavor, that our hearts be whole from sins; and when it happens that we are scourged in the body, let us pray to Him for relief.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul besought Him that He would take away the thorn in his flesh, and He would not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he disturbed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was he filled with sadness, and did he speak of himself as deserted?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather did he say that he was not deserted, because that was not taken away which he desired to be taken away, to the end that infirmity might be cured.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this he found in the voice of the Physician, “My grace is sufficient for thee; for my strength is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence knowest thou, then, that God does not wish to heal thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As yet it is expedient for thee to be scourged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence knowest thou how diseased that is which the physician cuts, using his knife on the diseased parts?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does he not know the measure, what he is to do, and how far he is to do it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does the shrieking of him he cuts restrain the hands of the physician cutting according to his art?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The one cries, the other cuts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is he cruel who does not listen to the man crying out, or is he not rather merciful in following the wound, that he may heal the sick man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These things have I said, my brethren, in order that no one seek any other aid than that of God, when we happen to be under the reproof of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See that ye perish not; see that ye do not depart from the Lamb, and be devoured by the lion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have declared, then, why it was at the tenth hour.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see what follows: “One of the two which heard John speak, and followed Him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Messias, in Hebrew; Christ, in Greek; in Latin, Anointed. Χρῖσμα is anointing in Greek; Christ, therefore, is the Anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is peculiarly anointed, pre-eminently anointed; wherewith all Christians are anointed, He is pre-eminently anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear how He speaks in the psalm: “Wherefore God, Thy God, hath anointed Thee with the oil of gladness above Thy fellows.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all the holy ones are His fellows, but He in a peculiar sense is the Holy of Holies, peculiarly anointed, peculiarly Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he brought him to Jesus; and when Jesus beheld him, He said, Thou art Simon the son of Joannes: thou shall be called Cephas, which is, by interpretation, Peter.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not a great thing that the Lord said whose son Peter was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is great to the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knew all the names of His own saints, whom He predestinated before the foundation of the world; and dost thou wonder that He said to one man, Thou art the son of this man, and thou shall be called this or that?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it a great matter that He changed his name, and converted it from Simon to Peter?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter is from petra, a rock, but the petra [rock]; is the Church; in the name of Peter, then, was the Church figured.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is safe, unless he who builds upon the rock?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what saith the Lord Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that heareth these my words, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man building his house upon a rock” (he doth not yield to temptation).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The rain descended, the floods came, the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he that heareth my words, and doeth them not” (now let each one of us fear and beware), “I will liken him to a foolish man, who built his house upon the sand: the rain descended, the floods came, the winds blew, and beat upon that house; and it fell: and great was the fall of it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What profit is it to enter the Church for him who builds upon the sand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, by hearing and not doing, he builds indeed, but on the sand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he hears nothing, he builds nothing; but if he hears, he builds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we ask, Where?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he hears and does, he builds upon the rock; if he hears and does not, he builds upon the sand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are two kinds of builders, those building upon the rock, and those building upon the sand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, are those who do not hear?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are they safe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does He say that they are safe because they do not build?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are naked beneath the rains, before the winds, before the floods; when these come, they carry away those persons before they overthrow the houses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is then the only security, both to build, and to build upon the rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou wilt hear and do not, thou buildest; but thou buildest a ruin: and when temptation comes it overthrows the house, and carries away thee with the ruin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if thou dost not hear, thou art naked; thou thyself art dragged away by those temptations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear, then, and do; it is the only remedy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many, perchance, on this day, by hearing and not doing, are hurried away on the stream of this festival!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, through hearing and not doing, the flood cometh, this annual festival; the torrent is filled, it will pass away and become dry, but woe to him whom it shall carry away!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Know this, then, beloved, that unless a man hears and does, he builds not upon the rock, and he does not belong to that great name which the Lord so commended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He has called thy attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if Simon had been called Peter before, thou wouldest not have so clearly seen the mystery of the rock, and thou wouldest have thought that he was called so by chance, not by the providence of God; therefore God willed that he should be called first something else, that by the very change of name the reality of the sacrament might be commended to our notice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the day following He would go forth into Galilee, and finding Philip, He saith unto him, Follow me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now he was of the city of Andrew and Peter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Philip findeth Nathanael” (Philip who had been already called by the Lord); “and he said unto him, We have found Him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus, the son of Joseph.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was called the son of that man to whom His mother had been espoused.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that He was conceived and born while she was still a virgin, all Christians know well from the Gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Philip said to Nathanael, and he added the place, “from Nazareth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Nathanael said unto him, “From Nazareth something good can come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the meaning, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not as some read, for it is likewise wont to be read, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the words of Philip follow, who says, “Come and see.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the words of Philip can suitably follow both readings, whether you read it thus, as confirming, “From Nazareth something good can come,” to which Philip replies, “Come and see;” or whether as doubting, and making the whole a question, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come and see.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since then, whether read in this manner or in that, the words following are not incompatible, it is for us to inquire which of the two interpretations we shall adopt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of a man this Nathanael was, we prove by the words which follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what sort of a man he was; the Lord Himself bears testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great is the Lord, known by the testimony of John; blessed Nathanael, known by the testimony of the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the Lord, although He had not been commended by the testimony of John, Himself to Himself bore testimony, because the truth is sufficient for its own testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because men were not able to receive the truth, they sought the truth by means of a lamp, and therefore John was sent to show them the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the Lord bearing testimony to Nathanael: “Nathanael said unto him, Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Philip says to him, Come and see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus sees Nathanael coming to Him, and says concerning him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great testimony!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not of Andrew, nor of Peter, nor of Philip was that said which was said of Nathanael, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do we then, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ought this man to be the first among the apostles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not only is Nathanael not found as first among the apostles, but he is neither the middle nor the last among the twelve, although the Son of God bore such testimony to him, saying, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is the reason asked for?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In so far as the Lord intimates, we find a probable reason.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we ought to understand that Nathanael was learned and skilled in the law and for that reason was the Lord unwilling to place him among His disciples, because He chose unlearned persons, that He might by them confound the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the apostle speaking these things: “For ye see,” saith he, “your calling, brethren, how that not many wise men after the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are called: but God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty; and base things of the world, and things that are despised, hath God chosen, yea, and things which are not, as though they were things that are, to bring to nought things that are.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If a learned man had been chosen, perhaps he would have said that he was chosen for the reason that his learning made him worthy of choice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ, wishing to break the necks of the proud, did not seek the orator by means of the fisherman, but by the fisherman He gained the emperor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great was Cyprian as an orator, but before him was Peter the fisherman, by means of whom not only the orator, but also the emperor, should believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No noble was chosen in the first place, no learned man, because God chose the weak things of the world that He might confound the strong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This man, then, was great and without guile, and for this reason only was not chosen, lest the Lord should seem to any to have chosen the learned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And from this same learning in the law, it came that when he heard “from Nazareth,”—for he had searched the Scripture, and knew that the Saviour was to be expected thence, what the other scribes and Pharisees had difficulty in knowing,—this man, then, very learned in the law, when he heard Philip saying, “We have found Him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph;”—this man, who knew the Scriptures excellently well, when he heard the name “Nazareth,” was filled with hope, and said, “From Nazareth something good can come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us now see the rest concerning this man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “in whom is no guile?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps he had no sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps he was not sick?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps he did not need a physician?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No one is born here in such fashion as not to need that Physician.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is the meaning of the words, “in whom is no guile”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us search a little more intently—it will appear presently—in the name of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord says dolus [guile]; and every one who understands Latin knows that dolus is when one thing is done and another feigned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dolus (guile) is not dolor (pain).

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I say this because many brethren, not well skilled in Latin, so speak as to say, Dolus torments him, using it for dolor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dolus is fraud, it is deceit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When a man conceals one thing in his heart, and speaks another, it is guile, and he has, as it were, two hearts; he has, as it were, one recess of his heart where he sees the truth, and another recess where he conceives falsehood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that you may know that this is guile, it is said in the Psalms, “Lips of guile.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are “lips of guile”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It follows, “In a heart and in a heart have they spoken evil.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “in a heart and in a heart,” unless in a double heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, guile was not in Nathanael, the Physician judged him to be curable, not whole.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A whole man is one thing, a curable another, an incurable a third: he who is sick, but not hopelessly sick, is called curable; he who is sick hopelessly, incurable; but he who is already whole does not need a physician.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Physician, then, who had come to cure, saw that he was curable, because there was no guile in him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How was guile not in him, if he is a sinner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He confesses that he is a sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he is a sinner, and says that he is a just man, there is guile in his mouth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore in Nathanael He praised the confession of sin, He did not judge that he was not a sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, when the Pharisees, who seemed righteous to themselves, blamed the Lord, because, as physician, he mixed with the sick, and when they said, “Behold with whom he eats, with publicans and sinners,” the Physician replied to the madmen, “They that are whole need not a physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is to say, because you call yourselves righteous when you are sinners, because you judge yourselves to be whole when you are languishing, you put away from you the medicine, and do not hold fast health.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence that Pharisee who had asked the Lord to dinner, was whole in his own eyes; but that sick woman rushed into the house to which she had not been invited, and, made impudent by the desire of health, approached not the head of the Lord, nor the hands, but the feet; washed them with tears, wiped them with her hair, kissed them, anointed them with ointment,—made peace, sinner as she was, with the footprints of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Pharisee who sat at meat there, as though whole himself, blamed the Physician, and said within himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have known what woman touched his feet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He suspected that He knew not, because He did not repulse her to prevent His being touched with unclean hands; but He did know, He permitted Himself to be touched, that the touch itself might heal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, seeing the heart of the Pharisee, put forth a parable: “There was a certain creditor, which had two debtors; the one owed five hundred denars, and the other fifty; and when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which of them loved him most?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answered, “I suppose, Lord, he to whom he forgave most.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And turning to the woman, He said unto Simon, “Seest thou this woman?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet; but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped them with the hairs of her head: thou gavest me no kiss; she hath not ceased to kiss my feet: thou gavest me no oil; she hath anointed my feet with ointment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, I say unto thee, to her are forgiven many sins, for she loved much; but to whom little is forgiven, the same loveth little.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is to say, thou art more sick, but thou thinkest thyself whole; thou thinkest that little is forgiven thee when thou owest more.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well did she, because guile was not in her, deserve medicine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means, guile was not in her?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She confessed her sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This He also praises in Nathanael, that guile was not in him; for many Pharisees who abounded in sins said that they were righteous, and brought guile with them, which made it impossible for them to be healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus then saw this man in whom was no guile, and said, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nathanael saith unto Him, “Whence knowest Thou me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered and said, “Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig (that is, under the fig-tree), I saw thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nathanael answered and said unto Him, “Rabbi, Thou art the Son of God; Thou art the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some great thing Nathanael may have understood in the saying, “When thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee, before that Philip called thee;” for his words, “Thou art the Son of God, Thou art the King of Israel,” were not dissimilar to those of Peter so long afterwards, when the Lord said unto him, “Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona, for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there He named the rock, and praised the strength of the Church’s support in this faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here already Nathanael says, “Thou art the Son of God; Thou art the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it was said to him, “Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We must inquire whether this fig-tree signifies anything.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen, my brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We find the fig-tree cursed because it had leaves only, and not fruit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the beginning of the human race, when Adam and Eve had sinned, they made themselves girdles of fig leaves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Fig leaves then signify sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nathanael then was under the fig-tree, as it were under the shadow of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord saw him, he concerning whom it was said, “They that sat under the shadow of death, unto them hath light arisen.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then was said to Nathanael?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest to me, O Nathanael, “Whence knowest thou me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even now thou speakest to me, because Philip called thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He whom an apostle had already called, He perceived to belong to His Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O thou Church, O thou Israel, in whom is no guile! if thou art the people, Israel, in whom is no guile, thou hast even now known Christ by His apostles, as Nathanael knew Christ by Philip.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But His compassion beheld thee before thou knewest Him, when thou wert lying under sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For did we first seek Christ, and not He seek us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did we come sick to the Physician, and not the Physician to the sick?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not that sheep lost, and did not the shepherd, leaving the ninety and nine in the wilderness, seek and find it, and joyfully carry it back on his shoulders?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not that piece of money lost, and the woman lighted the lamp, and searched in the whole house until she found it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when she had found it, “Rejoice with me,” she said to her neighbors, “for I have found the piece of money which I lost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner were we lost as the sheep, lost as the piece of money; and our Shepherd found the sheep, but sought the sheep; the woman found the piece of money, but sought the piece of money.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the woman?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the lamp?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have prepared a lamp for my Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore were we sought that we might be found; having been found, we speak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not be proud, for before we were found we were lost, if we had not been sought.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them then not say to us whom we love, and whom we desire to gain to the peace of the Catholic Church, “What do you wish with us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why seek you us if we are sinners?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We seek you for this reason that you perish not: we seek you because we were sought; we wish to find you because we have been found.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, then, Nathanael had said “Whence knowest Thou me?” the Lord said to him, “Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O thou Israel without guile, whosoever thou art; O people living by faith, before I called thee by my apostles, when thou wast under the shadow of death, and thou sawest not me, I saw thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord then says to him, “Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig-tree, thou believest: thou shalt see a greater thing than these.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, thou shalt see a greater thing than these?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He saith unto him, “Verily, verily, I say unto you, ye shall see heaven open, and angels ascending and descending upon the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Brethren, this is something greater than “under the fig-tree I saw thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is more that the Lord justified us when called than that He saw us lying under the shadow of death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what profit would it have been to us if we had remained where He saw us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Should we not be lying there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this greater thing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When have we seen angels ascending and descending upon the Son of man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already on a former occasion I have spoken of these ascending and descending angels; but lest you should have forgotten, I shall speak of the latter briefly by way of recalling it to your recollection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I should use more words if I were introducing, not recalling the subject.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jacob saw a ladder in a dream; and on a ladder he saw angels ascending and descending: and he anointed the stone which he had placed at his head.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard that the Messias is Christ; you have heard that Christ is the Anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Jacob did not place the stone, the anointed stone, that he might come and adore it: otherwise that would have been idolatry, not a pointing out of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was done was a pointing out of Christ, so far as it behoved such a pointing out to be made, and it was Christ that was pointed out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A stone was anointed, but not for an idol.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A stone anointed; why a stone?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, I lay in Zion a stone, elect, precious: and he that believeth on Him shall not be confounded.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why anointed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because Christus comes from chrisma.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what saw he then on the ladder?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ascending and descending angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So it is the Church, brethren: the angels of God are good preachers, preaching Christ; this is the meaning of, “they ascend and descend upon the Son of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do they ascend, and how do they descend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In one case we have an example; listen to the Apostle Paul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What we find in him, let us believe regarding the other preachers of the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold Paul ascending: “I know a man in Christ fourteen years ago was caught up into the third heaven (whether in the body, or whether out of the body, I cannot tell: God knoweth), and that he heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard him ascending, hear him descending: “I could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal; as babes in Christ I have fed you with milk, not with meat.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold he descended who had ascended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask whether he ascended to the third heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask whether he descended to give milk to babes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear that he descended: “I became a babe in the midst of you, even as a nurse cherisheth her children.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we see both nurses and mothers descend to babes, and although they be able to speak Latin, they shorten the words, shake their tongues in a certain manner, in order to frame childish endearments from a methodical language; because if they speak according to rule, the infant does not understand nor profit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if there be a father well skilled in speaking, and such an orator that the forum resounds with his eloquence, and the judgment-seats shake, if he have a little son, on his return home he puts aside the forensic eloquence to which he had ascended, and in child’s language descends to his little one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear in one place the apostle himself ascending and descending in the same sentence: “For whether,” says he,“we be beside ourselves, it is to God; or whether we be sober, it is for your cause.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “we are beside ourselves”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That we see those things which it is not lawful for a man to speak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “we are sober for your cause?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have I judged myself to know anything among you, save Jesus Christ and Him crucified?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the Lord Himself ascended and descended, it is evident that His preachers ascend by imitation, descend by preaching.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if we have detained you somewhat longer than is our wont, the design was that the dangerous hours might pass: we imagine that those people have now brought their vanity to a close.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But let us, brethren, having fed upon the feasts of salvation, do what remains, that we may in a religious manner fill up the Lord’s day with spiritual joys, and compare the joys of verity with the joys of vanity; and if we are horrified, let us grieve; if we grieve, let us pray; if we pray, may we be heard; if we are heard, we gain them also.
